calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-07-289549 Conservatorship of Roosevelt Thompson and PTR-08-291053 The Roosevelt Thompson Trust.

Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Austin Thompson (Conservator/Trustee).

The Court stated that there are many questions raised regarding the accounting and that it could have been more detailed. Ms. Navia stated that the Conservatee was having some medical issues which caused him to be hospitalized, that her Client also had medical issues of his own and that things are now settled. Mr. Thompson stated that his Father is at Victoria Manor, that he (Austin) is staying in the basement unit, spoke of his record keeping experience as well as his professional experience and stated that there are three accounts. Ms. Navia explained that her Client inadvertently ordered checks in the Trust account instead of the conservatorship, that there were issues with the bank when ordering checks (fewer checks per order) and that these are things her Client will make sure does not happen again. Ms. Navia also stated that her Client's car broke down, that he used this car to take his Father on outings and that this is why they utilized the zip car. The Court stated that it does not have a problem with the use of the zip car, but more information should have been provided as far as where, when and what the outings were for. Mr. Thompson stated that $750.00 a month for the basement unit is a reasonable value and that he has lived there since 2016. Ms. Navia stated that Austin moved in to be closer to his Father and to deal with issues of non-tenants living there, noise complaints and other issues regarding the property, that $750.00 is de minimis in terms of the quantity and quality of services he provides, and that he is only requesting $2,000.00 for fees. The Sixth Account of Conservator, etc. and Sixth Account of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

Appearances: Tricia Friedeberg for petitioner Austin Thompson

Ms. Friedeberg stated that the trustee has been very ill, and while willing to help he has been unable to do so because of his condition. She requested a four week continuance in order to complete the accounting. The Court responded that if Mr. Thompson is not healthy enough to perform his duties as trustee, it may be necessary to suspend his powers and appoint a new trustee. Ms. Friedeberg requested that the Court wait to suspend powers until after the requested four weeks hve passed. The matter was continued to April 9, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Professional Market Analysis Of The Warehouse, Sale Of The Vehicles, Information Regarding The Agreement And Reimbursement By Austin Of The Disallowed Expenses ($4,297.20)
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Continued To Aug-06-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-07-289549 The Roosevelt Thompson Trust.

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson. Janet Boessenecker Court appointed Counsel for Conservatee.

Ms. Gordon stated that the buyer for the cadillac backed out, that the other vehicles are not operative, that the Conservator cannot locate the pink slips, that pink slips and back registration fees are needed in order to donate them and that they received another offer of $400.00 for the cadillac. Ms. Gordon also stated that income only goes into the conservatorship, that there will be no income coming into the Trust and that only the proceeds from the sale of the warehouse will go into the Trust. Furthermore, Ms. Gordon stated that payment for the care facility will alternate monthly between the Conservatorship and the Trust, that rents will go into the Conservatorship, and that insurance and taxes will be paid out of the Conservatorship. The Court raised the issue of ambiguity and problems with the way the stipualtion is drafted with regard to future expenses. The matters were continued to August 6, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Gordon is to file a petition in the Conservatorship matter to get approval of the agreement with respect to Austin's sharing of expenses of the Martin Luther King property as well as a petition in both the Conservatorship matter and the Trust matter to get approval of the agreement with respect to Roosevelt Sr.'s living expenses for all Parties to receive notice.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1807 Chestnut Street Oakland, Ca.)
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson.

The property sold for $300,000.00 (100% interest). The Court is confirming 50% of the interest sold based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $132,000.00 is required. A stipulation and order with regard to the Chestnut Street property expenses has been presented and will be signed upon review. The status hearing on that matter was taken off calendar. Ms. Gordon stated that Austin has agreed to sell the warehouse property, that it is in the process of being appraised and that everyone was in agreement.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Stipulation And Order As To What The Expenses Are
Off Calendar May-21-2012 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Professional Market Analysis Of The Warehouse, Sale Of The Vehicles, Information Regarding The Agreement And Reimbursement By Austin Of The Disallowed Expenses ($4,297.20)
Off Calendar Apr-30-2012 Continued To Jun-25-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-07-289549 Conservatorship of Roosevelt Thompson.

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson. Janet Boessenecker for Conservatee.

Ms. Gordon stated that she was able to file a stipulation regarding ownership of the Trust property, that once the property is sold that the Co-Owner of the property will no longer be involved in the matter, that the calculated expenses paid by the Conservatorship are $11,768.38, that their contribution is $5,239.39 to date and that the amount will be deducted from the Co-Owner's one half of the sale. In addition, Ms. Gordon stated that one automoblie sold for $700.00, that there were no offers on the other two automobiles, that they will either sell them for scrap or donate them and that the $4,200.00 has not been reimbursed. Furthermore, Ms. Gordon stated that she will execute a promissory note and have a date certain as to when it will be paid with an interest rate of 5%. Both matters were continued to June 25, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the following: 1) reimbursement of or a signed promissory note for the $4,297.00 expenses which is to be done by May 7, 2012 2) completion of an analysis of what to do with the warehouse 3) disposition of the remaining automobiles 4) resolution of Austin's obligation as to the warehouse expenses 5) resolution of the sharing of living expenses as between the two Estates. Status in the Trust matter as to filing of a stipulation and order as to what the expenses are was set for May 21, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Boessenecker is to prepare an order after hearing reflecting the above.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-23-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-07-289549 Conservatorship of Roosevelt Thompson.

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson (Conservator/Trustee). Janet Boessenecker for Conservatee.

The Court expressed concern about the lack of record keeping/receipts with regard to expenses of the Martin Luther King Way property (warehouse), the Conservatee's living expenses, the Chestnut Street property, what the true expenses are to be paid by other Co-Owners, etc. Ms. Gordon explained the reasons for maintaining both the Conservatorship and the Trust, addressed all of the issues raised by the Court as well as the Conservatee's current placement, and stated that there was an agreement between her Client and Mr. Park's Client as to the sharing of living expenses between the Conservatorship and the Trust. Ms. Gordon will provide a copy of the agreement to the Court. Both accountings are approved except for the Conservator's requests for reimbursement of those amounts ($4,297.20) that have not now been documented. The order does not resolve and leaves open the requirement of Co-Owners of the properties in the respective estates to make contibution to expenses as well as allocation of expenses between the two Estates. Those issues are not approved at this time. The Petitioner will petition to sell the vehicles and a professional market analysis will be done with respect to the Martin Luther King Way property. Ms. Gordon will submit revised orders. A status hearing on the professional market analysis of the warehouse, sale of the vehicles, information regarding the agreement and reimbursement by Austin of the disallowed expenses ($4,297.20) was set for April 30, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-07-289549 Conservatorship of Roosevelt Thompson.

Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Gordon and Mr. Thompson addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes. In addition, the Parties stated that the Martin Luther King warehouse property was being renovated so that it could be rented, that the renovations would take about two and a half months to complete and that the Chestnut property is going to be sold. Ms. Gordon also stated that she just received the sale contract, that the property had just been re-appraised, that a portion of the rental income would be used to defer Austin's share of expenses, that Austin is completing the insurance claim form for the theft from the warehouse and that the Conservatee does not need an allowance. The Court granted the accounting petitions on both matters (subject to review of the orders). Status of rental of the warehouse and sale of the Chestnut Street property was set for April 19, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park for Roosevelt Thompson (Objector). Mary Jane Gordon for and with Austin Thompson (Petitioner).

Mr. Park stated that his objections had been satisfied, that he was withdrawing his objections and that Ms. Gordon's Client was willing to resign as Trustee. Ms. Gordon confirmed that the amount of the fifty percent of the net proceeds should be changed to $3,944.80. The Court approved the accounting and will sign the order, (subject to the change being made).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.
